meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't collaborate (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't collaborate. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't collaborate
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and overcome collaboration blockers. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: To solve collaboration friction, implement a tiered SaaS pricing model that restricts team access to specific plans. By gating collaborative features—like shared workspaces or real-time editing—behind a Pro tier, you incentivize value-based upgrades while ensuring your architecture remains clean, scalable, and monetized from day one.
content.intro:
The screen stares back, a hollow void of unfinished code. You’ve built the feature, but your users remain isolated, unable to bridge the gap between their work and their team. The lack of collaborative infrastructure isn't just a technical hurdle; it’s a bottleneck that kills growth and leaves your portfolio project feeling like a static demo rather than a living product.

But what if your architecture was the catalyst for connection? By integrating intelligent SaaS pricing, you don't just monetize—you design a gateway that encourages users to invite others, turning a solitary tool into a collaborative engine. We will use Antigravity and Claude Code to architect a system that scales as fast as your user base.

steps.title: Define the Value Gate
steps.description: Identify which feature creates the 'collaborative' bottleneck. We will wrap this in a conditional check that forces a pricing tier upgrade.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a feature-gating service in TypeScript that checks user subscription status against a 'collaboration_enabled' flag in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a middleware file for feature gating based on subscription tiers'

steps.title: Schema Design for Teams
steps.description: Setup a relational structure that links user IDs to team IDs, allowing for granular permission control.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application where users belong to teams, and teams are assigned a specific pricing tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Update database schema to support team-based multi-tenancy'

steps.title: Stripe Integration logic
steps.description: Connect your application to Stripe to manage subscription events that toggle your collaboration features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler that updates the 'subscription_tier' field in my database when a user upgrades to the Pro plan.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement Stripe webhook handler for subscription lifecycle management'

steps.title: Frontend UI/UX Guardrails
steps.description: Implement front-end components that gracefully show a 'Upgrade to Collaborate' modal instead of broken UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component that detects a restricted action and displays an upgrade prompt if the user is on the Free tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Build upgrade-prompt modal component for restricted features'

steps.title: Testing the Workflow
steps.description: Simulate the upgrade journey to ensure the transition from 'no access' to 'collaboration enabled' is seamless.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write an end-to-end test script using Playwright to verify that a Free user cannot access the team dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Write Playwright test for subscription-based access control'

pro_tips: Always use a 'soft' gate on the frontend and a 'hard' gate on the backend to prevent API-level bypasses.
Offer a 14-day free trial on the Pro tier to allow users to experience collaboration before hitting the paywall.
Track 'Feature Usage' events to identify which users are most likely to upgrade based on their collaboration intent.
Use environment variables for your Stripe keys and never hardcode them in your client-side repository.
Keep your pricing logic decoupled from your core business logic to allow for rapid pivot iterations.
content.next_steps: Deploy your staging environment to Vercel or Railway and perform a live end-to-end test of the payment flow to ensure your database triggers correctly update user permissions.
content.conversion_block: Stop building tools that sit idle. Start building engines that drive growth, revenue, and connection. Your code is the foundation, but your strategy is the leverage. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
